items.headline: Tech Sell-off Drags Major Indices Lower Amid Inflation Fears
items.summary: U.S. major indices experienced a broad decline on May 17, 2026, as technology stocks led a sell-off. Concerns over persistent inflation, indicated by April's higher-than-expected CPI data, and the reduced likelihood of Federal Reserve rate cuts fueled negative market sentiment. Despite the daily downturn, the S&P 500 managed to secure its seventh consecutive weekly gain.

items.headline: P3 Health Partners Inc. leads monthly gains
items.summary: P3 Health Partners Inc. was the top-performing stock for May 2026, demonstrating significant monthly gains.

items.headline: Rackspace Technology sees strong monthly rise
items.summary: Rackspace Technology Inc. posted substantial monthly gains as of May 17, 2026.

items.headline: Agilon Health continues upward trend
items.summary: Agilon Health Inc. showed strong performance, making it one of the top monthly gainers.

items.headline: Nvidia drops as tech sector faces pressure
items.summary: Nvidia experienced a notable decline as technology stocks led the market downturn, with investors reacting to rising yields.

items.headline: Intel falls amid broader tech sell-off
items.summary: Intel shares fell significantly as the tech sector broadly declined following inflation data.

items.headline: SoundHound AI impacted by CPI report
items.summary: SoundHound AI shares dropped after the Consumer Price Index report exceeded expectations, affecting growth-oriented tech.

items.headline: April CPI Exceeds Expectations, Raising Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) for April showed an annual increase of 3.8%, surpassing economists' predictions. This persistent inflation reduces the likelihood of the Federal Reserve lowering interest rates, impacting growth sectors like technology.

items.headline: Jerome Powell Named Interim Fed Chair, Kevin Warsh Expected to Shift Policy
items.summary: Jerome Powell has been appointed interim Chairman of the U.S. Federal Reserve, serving until Kevin Warsh officially takes oath as the new Fed Chair. Warsh is anticipated to pursue a strategy of shrinking the Fed's balance sheet, a move that could significantly influence bond and stock markets, particularly by increasing borrowing costs.

items.headline: Tech Sector Leads Market Decline on Rate Hike Worries
items.summary: Technology and software stocks experienced a broad decline today. Extended periods of higher borrowing costs tend to diminish the present value of future earnings for growth-oriented sectors, leading to a reassessment of company valuations.

items.headline: U.S. and China Achieve Positive Outcomes in Trade Consultations
items.summary: Recent economic and trade consultations between China and the United States have yielded positive results. Both nations agreed to establish trade and investment councils, reduce tariffs on specific products, and address non-tariff barriers, aiming to promote two-way trade.

items.headline: Coca-Cola Reports Strong Q1 2026 Earnings, Driven by Fuze Tea Growth
items.summary: Coca-Cola ($KO) exceeded expectations in its Q1 2026 earnings report, demonstrating strong diversification beyond traditional soft drinks. The company's Fuze Tea brand was highlighted as a significant growth engine, capitalizing on global tea market expansion.

items.headline: PTC Exceeds Q1 2026 Forecasts with Strong CAD and PLM Adoption
items.summary: PTC exceeded Wall Street's Q1 2026 forecasts, reporting stronger-than-anticipated revenue and adjusted earnings per share. The company attributed its robust performance to accelerated customer adoption of its product lifecycle management (PLM) and computer-aided design (CAD) offerings.

items.headline: Energy and Commodities Favored in Recent Market Flows
items.summary: In the week leading up to May 17, market flows favored large-cap growth, energy, commodities, and cybersecurity sectors. Rising energy prices, partly due to geopolitical tensions, continue to be a significant factor influencing market sentiment and inflation expectations.
